metadata.seed: I am 50 and returning to school because I am out of work. I am, having a very tough time.

patient.query: Yeah, it’s been pretty overwhelming. I thought going back to school would help me feel more in control, but instead, I just feel this constant weight on my shoulders. The pressure to keep up with everything—assignments, grades, and then thinking about finding a job—it's just a lot to handle. Sometimes I wonder if I even made the right choice coming back.

triggers: pressure to keep up with assignments

I just feel this constant weight on my shoulders
it's just a lot to handle

patient.query: Yeah, exactly. I thought I’d be excited to learn and push myself, but instead, it feels like I’m just trying to survive the day. I keep comparing myself to others, and it makes things worse. I worry they’re all doing better than I am, and that just makes me want to hide away more.

automatic_thoughts: I should be excited
I'm just trying to survive
They're all doing better than I am

patient.query: It really is frustrating. I just thought it would be different, you know? Everyone talks about how school can be this amazing experience, but I just feel so behind and like I’m not good enough. I keep telling myself to just tough it out, but some days, I just want to give up.

I'm not good enough
just tough it out
I want to give up

patient.query: I guess it’s just... I feel like I have so much more at stake. For me, it’s about proving that I can do this after taking a break. It feels like everyone else has their path figured out, and I’m just fumbling around trying to catch up. I can’t shake this feeling that if I mess up, it’ll be a huge setback—not just in school, but in my life. I don’t know if they really get how heavy that feels.

automatic_thoughts: I have so much more at stake
everyone else has their path figured out
I'm fumbling around trying to catch up
if I mess up, it’ll be a huge setback

patient.query: I think "proving" means getting good grades and participating in class, like really showing I belong there. I feel like I have to work twice as hard to convince not just my classmates, but myself too. If I don't excel, it feels like I’ll always be seen as the one who took time off, like I didn’t take it seriously or something. It’s like there's this constant need to validate my choices to everyone, including myself.

patient.query: Yeah, it’s tough to pin it down. I think a lot of it comes from my past experiences—like, in school before, I felt like I was always expected to be at the top. My family has high expectations, and I guess I've internalized that. It’s almost like I can't let anyone down, including myself, and that adds weight on top of everything else. I just keep thinking about what it means if I don’t meet those expectations.
